I’ve been using the NoiseFit Evolve 3 for about 1.5 years, and it has been a surprisingly reliable smartwatch for the price. I bought it for ₹1,799 (now it’s around ₹2,199). Here’s my real, long-term experience:
Display: Crisp and bright AMOLED screen — easily visible even in bright daylight.
Waterproofing: True IP68 rating. I’ve washed it with soap and a brush many times, even submerged it briefly, and it still works perfectly.
Durability: Survived multiple drops with only minor scratches; everything still functions as new.
Battery Life: When new, I got up to 14 days on minimal use. After 1.5 years, with Always-On Display, 24×7 heart rate, and SpO₂ tracking, it still gives a solid 5 days per charge — excellent battery consistency.
Bluetooth & Calling: Bluetooth connection is strong and stable, even with iPhones. Bluetooth calling works great — clear audio on both ends, and it’s still perfectly audible even in outdoor conditions.
Features: Covers all key health and fitness tracking options — heart rate, SpO₂, sleep, stress, sports modes, etc. It only lacks a calculator app, but that’s not really a practical need.
Build Quality: Solid metallic body, feels premium for its price, and buttons remain responsive even after long use.
